question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: External/guest access loss caused by a cloud vs on‑prem group/object collision and fixed by group consolidation + full Azure AD Connect sync
context:
=== CITED SOURCES ===
SOURCE 13:
﻿
Ticket summary:
Ticket name: KF25: SharePoint – External project members lose access sporadically
Ticket number: TI-1583327
Company: Customer Company 25 AG
Contact person: Contact Person 07
Owner: Employee 31
Origin: Email
Type: Incident
Created on: 07.04.25 08:12
Closure
Closure date: 17.04.25 15:18
Closure text: Root cause identified (Azure AD Connect synchronization error affecting group attributes). Cleanup and full sync performed. Access stable.
Activities
07.04.25 08:04
Subject: Project members (external) lose access to SharePoint
From: Contact Person 07
Good morning
Several external project members report that they no longer have access to the folder
/Projects/Compliance_Review_2025
Internal employees do not appear to have issues.
Please review.
Kind regards
Contact Person 07
07.04.25 08:12
Ticket created
Assigned to: Employee 31
Status: In Progress
Notes
07.04.25 09:05
Helpdesk asks:
• Does it affect only external users?
• What error message appears?
• Since when exactly?
• Were groups or permissions adjusted?
07.04.25 10:20
Customer responds:
Error message:
Access denied
One user could access yesterday.
Today no longer.
No deliberate changes made.
07.04.25 11:10
Helpdesk checks SharePoint:
• Folder permissions correct
• Group “SP_Compliance_Main” contains “COMPLIANCE_TEAM”
• External users are members of “COMPLIANCE_TEAM”
Comparison internal vs external users:
No visible difference.
08.04.25 08:45
Customer adds:
One external user was newly added and immediately had access.
Another who has been in the project for months loses access sporadically.
08.04.25 10:15
Helpdesk tests:
• Remove + re-add to group
→ Access temporarily restored
After approx. 1 hour, no access again.
08.04.25 14:30
Hypothesis:
Token / cache / Conditional Access
Test:
User fully logs out → logs in again
→ Access remains denied
Hypothesis rejected.
09.04.25 09:12
Helpdesk asks:
Are external users created as “Guest” or “Member”?
09.04.25 10:05
Customer:
All externals are created as Guest.
Some were recently synchronized from On-Prem AD to Cloud.
09.04.25 11:40
First observation:
For two affected users in Entra ID portal, attribute
“source” = Windows Server AD
For functioning external users:
“source” = Azure AD
Correlation unclear.
09.04.25 14:20
Ticket escalated for in-depth analysis.
Assigned Group changed to: BU Infrastructure – Identity
10.04.25 08:55
Ticket taken over by: System Engineer 14
In-depth analysis
10.04.25 09:40
Azure AD Connect reviewed.
Observation:
Group “COMPLIANCE_TEAM” exists both On-Prem and in Cloud.
On-Prem version is synchronized.
Cloud version was originally created manually.
Result:
• External guests exist only in Cloud group
• SharePoint references the synchronized On-Prem group
Same name, but different Object IDs.
10.04.25 11:25
Review of group members via Graph:
Cloud group:
→ Contains external guests
Synchronized On-Prem group:
→ Contains only internal users
Since SharePoint evaluates by Object ID, not by name, external users lose access once synchronization takes effect.
11.04.25 08:30
Reproduction in test:
• On-Prem group re-synchronized
• Cloud group overwritten
• External users reproducibly lose access
Root Cause
Two groups existed with identical name:
COMPLIANCE_TEAM
1. Cloud-native group
2. Synchronized On-Prem group
Through Azure AD Connect, the Cloud group was gradually overwritten or deactivated.
SharePoint referenced the synchronized version.
External guests were never managed On-Prem and therefore were not included in the synchronized group.
Actions
11.04.25 – 14.04.25
• Consolidation of group structure
• Old Cloud group removed
• On-Prem group properly cleaned
• External guests managed as Cloud-only members
• Azure AD Connect full synchronization performed
15.04.25 – Monitoring
Access remains stable
No further inconsistencies
17.04.25 15:18 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 07
The root cause was the parallel existence of two groups with identical names but different origins (Cloud vs. On-Prem synchronized).
Due to synchronization, effective group membership for external users was not correctly applied.
The group structure was consolidated and fully synchronized.
Access is now stable.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company

question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: Sales outbound dialing failing because a provisioning/script or voice‑routing‑policy assignment was incorrect and required policy/provisioning fixes (engineer involvement)
context:
=== CITED SOURCES ===
SOURCE 9:
﻿
Ticket summary:
Ticket name: KF52: Microsoft Teams – External number cannot be dialed
Ticket number: TI-1609823
Company: Customer Company 52 AG
Contact person: Contact Person 21
Owner: Employee 39
Origin: Email
Type: Incident
Created on: 03.09.25 09:08
Closure
Closure date: 12.09.25 15:40
Closure text: Analysis completed. Routing configuration adjusted according to Teams Voice Architecture. Dialing external numbers possible again.
Activities
03.09.25 08:55 – Email from Contact Person 21
Good morning
Some employees have been unable to dial external phone numbers since today.
Internal Teams calls function normally.
External calls are terminated immediately; in some cases the message “Call failed” appears.
Could you please check this?
Kind regards
03.09.25 09:08
Ticket created and assigned to Employee 39.
03.09.25 09:40 – Helpdesk inquiry
Good day
Are all users affected or only specific ones?
Does it affect all external numbers or only certain ones?
Were there any recent changes to licenses or user roles?
Thank you for your feedback.
03.09.25 10:25 – Customer response
It affects about 6 users, all from the Sales team.
Other teams can call normally.
It affects all external numbers.
We are not aware of any license changes.
03.09.25 11:15 – Internal note
Test performed with affected user.
Internal call → works.
External call → immediate termination after dialing.
Teams briefly shows “Connecting”, then “Call ended”.
04.09.25 08:50 – Email to customer
Good morning
We are currently reviewing the voice routing and license configuration.
Can you confirm whether these users were recently moved to another team or security group?
Kind regards
04.09.25 09:30 – Customer response
Yes, the affected users were organizationally reassigned to the new department “Enterprise Sales” last week.
New Azure AD groups were created for this purpose.
04.09.25 11:40 – Teams Admin Center analysis
Users have:
• Teams license
• Calling Plan not active
• Direct Routing configured
Observation:
Voice Routing Policy = “Global”
Not “DR-Sales”
Comparison with working user from another team shows:
Voice Routing Policy = “DR-Standard”
05.09.25 09:05 – Internal review
Get-CsOnlineVoiceRoutingPolicy shows that policy “Global” does not reference any PSTN Usage.
Policy “DR-Standard” includes PSTN Usage “External-Route”.
05.09.25 11:20 – Email to customer
Good day
We have identified that the affected users are currently assigned a different Voice Routing Policy than the other employees.
We are reviewing why this assignment was changed.
Kind regards
08.09.25 – Internal analysis
New Azure AD group “Enterprise-Sales” was created.
Automated provisioning script assigns policy “Global” by default when users become members of this group.
The internal document
“Blueprint – Teams Voice Routing & Policy Assignment – Version 1.3”
states that users with Direct Routing must be assigned a dedicated routing policy.
09.09.25 – Test phase
Temporary assignment of policy “DR-Standard” to affected user.
Test call to mobile phone performed.
Call successful.
Reverted to “Global” → issue reproducible.
10.09.25 – Coordination with Identity team
Provisioning script was modified two weeks ago.
New group structure was not reflected in the routing blueprint.
11.09.25 – Adjustment
Policy assignment corrected according to Voice Routing architecture.
Provisioning logic adjusted.
12.09.25 – Customer feedback
Good day
All affected users can dial external numbers again.
Longer calls also function normally.
Thank you for your support.
12.09.25 15:40 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 21
The root cause was an incorrect Voice Routing Policy assignment following an organizational restructuring.
The routing configuration was adjusted according to the internal Teams Voice Architecture.
External calls are now possible again.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company

question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: Finance users receiving the wrong site printers after a Finance-group nesting error; group membership/nesting fixes were applied (ticketed remediation)
context:
=== CITED SOURCES ===
SOURCE 2:
﻿
Ticket summary:
Ticket name: KF60: Incorrect printer automatically set as default
Ticket number: TI-1623017
Company: Customer Company 60 AG
Contact person: Contact Person 18
Owner: Employee 23
Origin: Email
Type: Incident
Created on: 18.11.25 08:49
Closure
Closure date: 24.11.25 15:11
Closure text: Printer assignment and default configuration corrected according to Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard. Behavior stable.
Activities
18.11.25 08:35 – Email from Contact Person 18
Good morning
Several employees in the Finance department report that their default printer automatically switches to “MFP-Zuerich-01”, although they work at the Basel location.
They must manually select the correct printer before each print job.
The issue seems to have started last week.
Kind regards
18.11.25 08:49
Ticket created and assigned to Employee 23.
18.11.25 09:20 – Initial analysis
Remote connection established with an affected user.
Default printer is indeed “MFP-Zuerich-01”.
Both Basel and Zurich printers are visible in the printer list.
Comparison with a user from another department shows the correct location printer set.
18.11.25 10:05 – Inquiry to customer
Good day
Were there any recent organizational changes or group adjustments in the Finance department?
Kind regards
18.11.25 10:40 – Customer response
Yes, the Finance team was moved into a new AD group last week.
The old group was deactivated.
19.11.25 09:10 – GPO assignment analysis
Comparison with “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard – Version 1.0”.
In the section Printer Provisioning it is defined that:
• Printer assignments are location-based via AD groups
• Default printers per location are set via GPO
• No parallel groups with identical function may exist
Review shows that the new Finance group is additionally a member of a higher-level Zurich location group.
As a result, the Zurich printer is also mapped via GPO and set as default.
19.11.25 11:30 – Test
Temporary removal of the incorrect group membership.
gpupdate /force executed.
User logged in again.
Default printer now correctly remains “MFP-Basel-01”.
20.11.25 – Coordination with customer
Confirmation that the group structure had not been fully cleaned up during the reorganization.
Correction performed according to blueprint specification.
24.11.25 14:50 – Customer feedback
Multiple users tested.
Default printer remains correct.
No further automatic switching.
24.11.25 15:11 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 18
The root cause was an inconsistent group membership following an organizational adjustment.
Printer assignment was corrected according to the “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard”.
The correct location printer now remains permanently set as default.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company

question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: SBC/Direct‑Routing incidents where missing translation entries or session‑timer/NAT mismatches required SBC/config and carrier coordination to resolve (temporary translation rule → permanent update; session‑timer changes)

Ticket name: KF52: Microsoft Teams – Incoming calls from specific numbers not received
Ticket number: TI-1612036
Company: Customer Company 52 AG
Contact person: Contact Person 22
Owner: Employee 41
Origin: Email
Type: Incident
Created on: 18.09.25 08:56
Closure
Closure date: 29.09.25 16:18
Closure text: Analysis completed. Routing and translation rules adjusted according to Direct Routing Blueprint. Incoming calls fully functional again.
Activities
18.09.25 08:40 – Email from Contact Person 22
Good morning
We have noticed that incoming calls from certain customers are no longer reaching us.
Callers report that they either receive a busy tone or the connection is disconnected after a few seconds.
Other external calls are functioning normally.
Could you please check this?
Kind regards
18.09.25 08:56
Ticket created and assigned to Employee 41.
18.09.25 09:25 – Helpdesk inquiry
Good day
Could you please provide the following information:
– Example phone numbers where the issue occurs
– Since when the issue has existed
– Whether all users are affected or only specific ones
Thank you.
18.09.25 10:15 – Customer response
Here are two examples:
+41 44 785 23 10
+41 44 785 23 11
Other numbers such as +41 31 … work without issue.
The problem has existed since last week.
All employees are affected.
18.09.25 11:40 – Internal analysis
Test call from private mobile (different number range) → works.
Test with number in specified range → call not delivered.
In the Teams Admin Center, no incoming call is shown.
19.09.25 09:05 – Initial hypothesis
Possible provider issue.
Carrier contacted.
19.09.25 14:20 – Carrier feedback
From the carrier’s perspective, the call is correctly delivered to the company’s SBC.
No routing errors visible on their side.
22.09.25 08:55 – SBC log analysis
For functioning numbers:
INVITE accepted and forwarded to Teams.
For affected numbers:
INVITE received but immediately answered with “404 Not Found”.
22.09.25 11:30 – Email to customer
Good day
We see that incoming calls from the provider are reaching the SBC.
However, the requests are not being correctly forwarded internally.
Were there recent changes to number ranges or new extensions?
Kind regards
22.09.25 13:05 – Customer response
Yes, we received a new number range from the provider last week and partially migrated old numbers.
The affected numbers belong to this new range.
23.09.25 09:10 – Internal review
Comparison with document
“Blueprint – Microsoft Teams Direct Routing & Number Translation – Version 2.3”
Section regarding:
• Normalization of incoming numbers
• E.164 format
• Translation rules on SBC
• Voice routing policies
Finding:
New numbers are not included in the SBC translation table.
Routing rule covers only the old number range.
24.09.25 – Test
Temporary adjustment of translation rule for new number range.
Test call performed.
Call correctly signaled and delivered.
Reverting to original configuration → issue reproducible.
25.09.25 – Coordination with customer
Confirmation that new number range was fully migrated.
Internal documentation had not yet been updated.
26.09.25 – Adjustment
Routing and translation rules extended according to architectural specification.
Details not documented in ticket for security reasons.
29.09.25 – Customer feedback
All tested numbers are working again.
Multiple customers also confirmed successful calls.
Thank you for the support.
29.09.25 16:18 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 22
The root cause was an inconsistent translation and routing configuration following the introduction of a new number range.
The configuration was adjusted according to the internal Direct Routing architecture.
Incoming calls are now correctly delivered again.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company

﻿
Ticket summary:
Ticket name: KF52: Microsoft Teams – External calls disconnect after approx. 30 seconds
Ticket number: TI-1604879
Company: Customer Company 52 AG
Contact person: Contact Person 16
Owner: Employee 35
Origin: Email
Type: Incident
Created on: 22.07.25 09:14
Closure
Closure date: 31.07.25 15:52
Closure text: Analysis completed. Adjustment made according to Direct Routing architecture. Calls stable.
Activities
22.07.25 09:02 – Email from Contact Person 16 to Helpdesk
Good morning
Since yesterday, several employees report that external calls in Microsoft Teams disconnect after approximately 30 seconds.
The call is established normally, both parties can hear each other, but the connection is terminated after a short time.
Internal Teams calls are not affected.
Could you please review this?
Kind regards
22.07.25 09:14
Ticket created and assigned to Employee 35.
22.07.25 10:05 – Helpdesk inquiry
Good day
Thank you for your report.
Could you please confirm:
– Does it affect incoming and outgoing calls?
– Does the disconnection always occur after approximately the same duration?
– Are all locations affected or only a specific one?
Thank you very much.
22.07.25 10:42 – Customer response
It only affects the Basel location.
Both incoming and outgoing calls.
The disconnection occurs almost exactly after 30 seconds, sometimes slightly earlier.
Home office users are not affected.
22.07.25 11:30 – Internal note
Test performed with affected user.
External call to mobile phone.
Call disconnected after 29–31 seconds.
No error message in the Teams client, simply “Call Ended.”
23.07.25 08:55 – Initial hypothesis
Possible client error or unstable connection.
Test with another device → same behavior.
Test with LAN cable instead of docking station → no change.
23.07.25 11:10 – Email to customer
Good day
We are currently analyzing the Direct Routing logs.
Signaling is established correctly.
The disconnection does not appear to be triggered by the client.
Were there any recent network changes at the Basel location?
Kind regards
23.07.25 13:45 – Customer response
On Friday, a new network segment for VoIP traffic was implemented.
A separate VLAN for “Voice” was created.
Could this be relevant?
24.07.25 09:05 – Internal analysis
SBC logs reviewed:
– SIP INVITE successful
– 200 OK correct
– Call established
– After 30 seconds, BYE from SBC
No obvious error code.
24.07.25 10:30 – Firewall analysis
No blocking visible.
Ports open according to standard configuration.
24.07.25 14:20 – Detailed SIP analysis
Session-Expires header reviewed.
Session timer set to 30 seconds.
Comparison with other locations shows:
There, 180 seconds.
25.07.25 08:50 – Review of internal documentation
Document:
“Blueprint – Microsoft Teams Direct Routing & Session Handling – Version 2.1”
Section on NAT session persistence and SIP session timers relevant.
Note in the blueprint:
For certain firewall or NAT configurations, the session timer must be adjusted to avoid premature termination.
25.07.25 11:15 – Internal tests
Temporary adjustment of session parameters on SBC.
Test call performed.
Call remains stable for several minutes.
Reverting to original configuration → disconnection after approx. 30 seconds reproducible.
28.07.25 – Coordination with network team
New “Voice” VLAN uses different NAT policy.
UDP session timeout configured to 30 seconds.
Comparison with Zurich location → 180 seconds.
29.07.25 – Adjustment
NAT session timeout adjusted according to architecture specification.
Details not documented in ticket for security reasons.
30.07.25 – Customer feedback
Good morning
We conducted several test calls.
No more disconnections observed.
Longer conversations are also stable.
Thank you very much.
31.07.25 15:52 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 16
The root cause was a deviation in the session and NAT configuration at the Basel location related to the new VLAN structure.
The configuration was adjusted according to the internal Direct Routing architecture.
Calls now remain stable.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company

question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: The reported symptoms span identity/provisioning (freelancers locked out of shared workspaces), Unified Communications/Direct Routing (dialer beeps and immediate drop), and site/GPO/print mapping (invoices printing at the wrong office). In the indexed knowledge base, the same pattern — simultaneous, reproducible failures across identity, telephony, and print routing after an org/group change — repeatedly required specialist investigation and hands‑on engineering changes (Azure AD Connect/group consolidation, voice routing policy or SBC config edits, GPO/printer membership corrections). Those prior tickets were not resolved by routine first‑line runbook steps but by second‑level teams (identity/infra, UC/SBC engineers, Core Engineering)

Ticket summary:
Ticket name: KF25: SharePoint – External project members lose access sporadically
Ticket number: TI-1583327
Company: Customer Company 25 AG
Contact person: Contact Person 07
Owner: Employee 31
Origin: Email
Type: Incident
Created on: 07.04.25 08:12
Closure
Closure date: 17.04.25 15:18
Closure text: Root cause identified (Azure AD Connect synchronization error affecting group attributes). Cleanup and full sync performed. Access stable.
Activities
07.04.25 08:04
Subject: Project members (external) lose access to SharePoint
From: Contact Person 07
Good morning
Several external project members report that they no longer have access to the folder
/Projects/Compliance_Review_2025
Internal employees do not appear to have issues.
Please review.
Kind regards
Contact Person 07
07.04.25 08:12
Ticket created
Assigned to: Employee 31
Status: In Progress
Notes
07.04.25 09:05
Helpdesk asks:
• Does it affect only external users?
• What error message appears?
• Since when exactly?
• Were groups or permissions adjusted?
07.04.25 10:20
Customer responds:
Error message:
Access denied
One user could access yesterday.
Today no longer.
No deliberate changes made.
07.04.25 11:10
Helpdesk checks SharePoint:
• Folder permissions correct
• Group “SP_Compliance_Main” contains “COMPLIANCE_TEAM”
• External users are members of “COMPLIANCE_TEAM”
Comparison internal vs external users:
No visible difference.
08.04.25 08:45
Customer adds:
One external user was newly added and immediately had access.
Another who has been in the project for months loses access sporadically.
08.04.25 10:15
Helpdesk tests:
• Remove + re-add to group
→ Access temporarily restored
After approx. 1 hour, no access again.
08.04.25 14:30
Hypothesis:
Token / cache / Conditional Access
Test:
User fully logs out → logs in again
→ Access remains denied
Hypothesis rejected.
09.04.25 09:12
Helpdesk asks:
Are external users created as “Guest” or “Member”?
09.04.25 10:05
Customer:
All externals are created as Guest.
Some were recently synchronized from On-Prem AD to Cloud.
09.04.25 11:40
First observation:
For two affected users in Entra ID portal, attribute
“source” = Windows Server AD
For functioning external users:
“source” = Azure AD
Correlation unclear.
09.04.25 14:20
Ticket escalated for in-depth analysis.
Assigned Group changed to: BU Infrastructure – Identity
10.04.25 08:55
Ticket taken over by: System Engineer 14
In-depth analysis
10.04.25 09:40
Azure AD Connect reviewed.
Observation:
Group “COMPLIANCE_TEAM” exists both On-Prem and in Cloud.
On-Prem version is synchronized.
Cloud version was originally created manually.
Result:
• External guests exist only in Cloud group
• SharePoint references the synchronized On-Prem group
Same name, but different Object IDs.
10.04.25 11:25
Review of group members via Graph:
Cloud group:
→ Contains external guests
Synchronized On-Prem group:
→ Contains only internal users
Since SharePoint evaluates by Object ID, not by name, external users lose access once synchronization takes effect.
11.04.25 08:30
Reproduction in test:
• On-Prem group re-synchronized
• Cloud group overwritten
• External users reproducibly lose access
Root Cause
Two groups existed with identical name:
COMPLIANCE_TEAM
1. Cloud-native group
2. Synchronized On-Prem group
Through Azure AD Connect, the Cloud group was gradually overwritten or deactivated.
SharePoint referenced the synchronized version.
External guests were never managed On-Prem and therefore were not included in the synchronized group.
Actions
11.04.25 – 14.04.25
• Consolidation of group structure
• Old Cloud group removed
• On-Prem group properly cleaned
• External guests managed as Cloud-only members
• Azure AD Connect full synchronization performed
15.04.25 – Monitoring
Access remains stable
No further inconsistencies
17.04.25 15:18 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 07
The root cause was the parallel existence of two groups with identical names but different origins (Cloud vs. On-Prem synchronized).
Due to synchronization, effective group membership for external users was not correctly applied.
The group structure was consolidated and fully synchronized.
Access is now stable.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company

Type: Incident

Closure
Closure date: 12.09.25 15:40

Activities
03.09.25 08:55 – Email from Contact Person 21
Good morning
Some employees have been unable to dial external phone numbers since today.
Internal Teams calls function normally.
External calls are terminated immediately; in some cases the message “Call failed” appears.
Could you please check this?
Kind regards
03.09.25 09:08
Ticket created and assigned to Employee 39.
03.09.25 09:40 – Helpdesk inquiry
Good day
Are all users affected or only specific ones?
Does it affect all external numbers or only certain ones?
Were there any recent changes to licenses or user roles?
Thank you for your feedback.
03.09.25 10:25 – Customer response
It affects about 6 users, all from the Sales team.
Other teams can call normally.
It affects all external numbers.
We are not aware of any license changes.
03.09.25 11:15 – Internal note

Internal call → works.
External call → immediate termination after dialing.
Teams briefly shows “Connecting”, then “Call ended”.
04.09.25 08:50 – Email to customer
Good morning
We are currently reviewing the voice routing and license configuration.
Can you confirm whether these users were recently moved to another team or security group?
Kind regards
04.09.25 09:30 – Customer response
Yes, the affected users were organizationally reassigned to the new department “Enterprise Sales” last week.
New Azure AD groups were created for this purpose.
04.09.25 11:40 – Teams Admin Center analysis

• Teams license
• Calling Plan not active
• Direct Routing configured

Voice Routing Policy = “Global”

Automated provisioning script assigns policy “Global” by default when users become members of this group.

“Blueprint – Teams Voice Routing & Policy Assignment – Version 1.3”
states that users with Direct Routing must be assigned a dedicated routing policy.
09.09.25 – Test phase

Reverted to “Global” → issue reproducible.
10.09.25 – Coordination with Identity team

11.09.25 – Adjustment
Policy assignment corrected according to Voice Routing architecture.
Provisioning logic adjusted.
12.09.25 – Customer feedback
Good day
All affected users can dial external numbers again.
Longer calls also function normally.
Thank you for your support.
12.09.25 15:40 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 21

The routing configuration was adjusted according to the internal Teams Voice Architecture.
External calls are now possible again.

Consulting Sample Company
